whenToUse: Use InfectiousAgentClass on an InfectiousDisease to declare the class of pathogen responsible — virus, bacteria, fungus, protozoa, multicellular parasite, or prion. Attach it via the `infectiousAgentClass` property so the disease entry exposes the etiologic class alongside the named pathogen on `infectiousAgent`.
whenNotToUse: Don't use InfectiousAgentClass to name a specific organism — that goes on `infectiousAgent` (e.g., "SARS-CoV-2"). Don't use it for non-infectious conditions, vector species, or environmental hazards; this enumeration is restricted to the six classes of transmissible biological agents.
whoItsFor: Public health agencies, hospital infection-prevention teams, travel-medicine publishers, and consumer health editors who maintain infectious-disease landing pages and want a clean facet for filtering by pathogen class.
seoNotes: Pathogen-class facets are a strong filter axis for AI assistants triaging symptom-to-pathogen questions. Pair with `infectiousAgent` for the named organism and `transmissionMethod` so the agent class, the organism, and the route are all machine-readable.
